text.title: Eval In Prod Sampling Policy Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Eval In Prod Sampling Policy must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Policy specifies the trigger condition, the named actor, and the deadline. Policy without all three is theatre.

rationale.source: Operational policy design (Charity Majors, Honeycomb essays)

rule.rationale: Operational policy design (Charity Majors, Honeycomb essays).

rule.statement: Policy is reviewed quarterly and the review is logged. Un-reviewed policies older than 4 quarters auto-suspend.
Policy violations are surfaced, not silenced. Each violation produces a ticket with named investigator.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Observability + drift detection rollout (3 weeks) Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
